---
title: "Julia Lucy Woodward"
---

## Julia Lucy Woodward

**Dates:** Cheltenham 1850-1910

### Description

probably a member of the Spider Society as \'Bath Brick\'

### Biography

Daughter of Richard Woodward (d.1875), retired Bengal Civil Service, she lived in Bath in 1871 and 1881
and 1891 with her widowed mother at the Knoll, Clevedon.  She was a fellow
of the Royal Meterological Society, a member of the Ex Libris Society
(collecting bookplates), and a supporter of local history societies and
publications. She contributed a memoir of her uncle Vincent Stuckey Lean to
his book on proverbs (1902-4). She compiled the autograph album in which the
letters to Bath Brick were found;  since Bath Brick is known to have lived
near Clevedon they were probably the same person.

---

[← Back to all letters](../letters.html)
